recording_notes: Phone filming monitor, 166.9s clip. First instrument visible at ~8.0s absolute; both ends cut at ~147.2s absolute; task time ~139.2s.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument and needle enter field; trainee begins orienting/loading needle before approaching Penrose.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup partially off-screen, costs time, no loss of control.

frame_analyses.description: Needle aligned to drain, first bite initiated through marked repair zone.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Acceptable angle, slightly tentative setup before drive.

frame_analyses.description: Suture traversed drain, being pulled through after second bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Close to marks, exact deviation hard to judge.

frame_analyses.description: First throw constructed above drain with visible double-wrap before seating.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right instrument leads; deliberate but not quick.

frame_analyses.description: Alternating single throw formed and brought down onto first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand lead switched; loop flatter and more compact.

frame_analyses.description: Final throw still being flattened after repeated regrasping and tension adjustments.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Least efficient segment; prolonged loop management.

frame_analyses.description: Blue cutter enters while grasper stabilizes knot to trim intracorporeally.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good stabilization; knot stays centered while tails cut.

frame_analyses.description: Instruments clear field; short tails remain, repaired slit approximated.

knot_assessments.notes: Likely double wrap, right-led; consistent with surgeon's knot, moderate confidence.
Left-led single alternating; seats without loosening.
Right-led single alternating; repeated regrasping to flatten, no gross slip.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Knot centered, closure complete, no reopening.

technique_summary: Task completed without avulsion or visible slippage; final closure complete with short tails. Bimanual control maintained. Efficiency dropped during the prolonged final throw due to regrasping and large loop management.
improvement_suggestions: Load needle and re-enter field with consistent 90-degree drive angle.
Shorten loop size and keep one strand under steady tension during the final throw.
Seat each throw flat onto drain earlier with smaller wrist motions.
strengths: Completed all required steps including intracorporeal cutting.
Final knot remains centered with good apposition, no residual gap.
Bimanual control maintained through pull-through and end cutting.
comparison_notes: LARGE time delta vs Claude V23 (Δ=15.8s). Claude read 155s total; GPT-4o read 139.2s (start at 8.0s absolute, end at 147.2s absolute). Same endpoint-definition pattern as V18. FLS delta 18.8 — second-widest in V-series after V18 (17.4). Both teachers converge on drain closure (no gap, no avulsion), knot sequence, and hand alternation; disagreement is purely on where the scoring clock stops.
